title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.imageAlt: Plate of crispy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken garnished with sesame seeds and green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Get ready to devour the most delicious homemade Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken, perfectly crispy and coated in a sweet, spicy, and savory sauce!

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring perfectly crispy fried chicken coated in a luscious, sticky, sweet, and spicy sauce. Each bite offers an irresistible crunch followed by a burst of savory, tangy, and subtly fiery flavors. This iconic Korean comfort food is a true crowd-pleaser, perfect for sharing or enjoying as a satisfying meal.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with salt, black pepper, ginger powder, garlic powder, and 1 tablespoon of soy sauce. Mix well to coat all pieces evenly. Let it marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature, or up to 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the potato starch, all-purpose flour, and the egg. Gradually add 1/2 cup of water, mixing until you achieve a smooth, thick batter. The consistency should be like pancake batter, able to cling to the chicken without being too runny. Adjust water if necessary.

instructions.title: First Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot or deep fryer to 350F (175C). Dip each marinated chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it is fully coated. Carefully place the battered chicken into the hot oil, frying in batches to avoid overcrowding. Fry for about 6-7 minutes until light golden brown and cooked through. Remove and place on a wire rack to drain.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken is frying, prepare the sauce. In a small saucepan, combine gochujang, 2 tablespoons soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey (or corn syrup), minced garlic, minced ginger, 1 teaspoon sesame oil, and 2 tablespoons of water (and optional ketchup). Bring to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly until the sauce thickens slightly, about 3-5 minutes. Remove from heat.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Crispy Texture
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 375F (190C). Carefully return the first-fried chicken pieces to the hot oil for a second fry. Fry for another 3-4 minutes, or until the chicken is deeply golden brown and extra crispy. This double-frying technique is key for achieving the signature Korean fried chicken crunch. Remove and drain on a wire rack.

instructions.title: Coat with Sauce and Serve
instructions.text: In a large bowl, add the freshly second-fried chicken. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss gently but thoroughly until all pieces are evenly coated. Garnish generously with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve immediately while hot and crispy.

tips: Always double-fry your chicken for the ultimate crispy texture. The first fry cooks the chicken, and the second fry, at a higher temperature, makes the skin incredibly crisp.
Using potato starch in the batter is traditional for Korean fried chicken, as it creates a lighter, airier, and crispier crust compared to all-purpose flour alone.
Adjust the spice level of the Yangnyeom sauce to your preference. For more heat, add a pinch of gochugaru (Korean chili powder). For a milder sauce, increase the amount of honey or ketchup.
article:
In the vibrant tapestry of global cuisine, few dishes have captured hearts and palates quite like Korean Fried Chicken, affectionately known as "KFC." But among its many delicious iterations, one stands out as a true icon: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken. This sweet, spicy, and incredibly savory glazed chicken is more than just a meal; it's a culinary experience, a testament to the art of flavor balance and texture perfection. If you've ever craved that irresistible crunch followed by a sticky, finger-licking sauce, you're in the right place. This guide will walk you through everything you need to know to master this beloved Korean Chicken Recipe in your own kitchen.

## What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?
Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken refers to fried chicken coated in a thick, glossy, and intensely flavorful sauce. The word "Yangnyeom" (양념) itself translates to "seasoned" or "sauce," perfectly describing the essence of this dish. Unlike its American counterpart, Korean fried chicken is renowned for its ultra-crispy, almost paper-thin crust, achieved through a unique double-frying method and a special batter. The Yangnyeom sauce is the star, typically a tantalizing blend of gochujang (Korean chili paste), soy sauce, garlic, ginger, sugar, and often a touch of vinegar or mirin. This creates a complex profile that is simultaneously spicy, sweet, tangy, and umami-rich, making it incredibly addictive. It's a staple among Korean Chicken Recipes, often enjoyed as a late-night snack (chimaek – chicken and beer) or a casual meal.

## Ingredients for Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken
Crafting the perfect Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken requires attention to both the chicken preparation and the sauce. Here's what you'll need:

**For the Chicken:**
*   2-3 lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces (wings, drumsticks, or thighs are ideal)
*   1 tsp salt
*   1/2 tsp black pepper
*   1/2 tsp garlic powder (optional)
*   1/2 tsp ginger powder (optional)
*   1/2 cup all-purpose flour
*   1/2 cup cornstarch or potato starch (crucial for crispiness)
*   1 tsp baking powder (for extra airy crunch)
*   1 cup cold water or sparkling water (for a lighter batter)
*   Vegetable oil, canola oil, or peanut oil for deep frying

**For the Yangnyeom Sauce:**
*   1/4 cup gochujang (Korean chili paste)
*   2 tbsp soy sauce
*   2 tbsp sugar (brown sugar or honey works well)
*   1 tbsp rice vinegar or apple cider vinegar
*   1 tbsp mirin (sweet rice wine, optional, for depth)
*   1 tbsp minced garlic
*   1 tsp minced ginger
*   1 tsp sesame oil
*   2 tbsp water or chicken broth
*   1 tsp sesame seeds (for garnish)
*   Chopped green onions or crushed peanuts (for garnish, optional)

## How to Make Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken: Step-by-Step

**Step 1: Prepare the Chicken**
Thoroughly clean and pat the chicken pieces very dry with paper towels. This is crucial for a crispy skin. Season the chicken with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and ginger powder.

**Step 2: Make the Batter**
In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch/potato starch, and baking powder. Gradually add the cold water (or sparkling water), whisking until a smooth, thin batter forms. It should be the consistency of pancake batter. Dip each piece of seasoned chicken into the batter, ensuring it's evenly coated but not too thick. Let excess batter drip off.

**Step 3: First Fry**
Heat about 2-3 inches of oil in a large pot or Dutch oven to 325-350°F (160-175°C). Carefully add chicken pieces in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 8-10 minutes, or until cooked through and lightly golden. Remove the chicken with tongs or a slotted spoon and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain. Let the chicken rest for at least 5-10 minutes.

**Step 4: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce**
While the chicken rests, combine all Yangnyeom sauce ingredients (gochujang, soy sauce, sugar, vinegar, mirin, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, water) in a small saucepan. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens slightly and becomes glossy. Remove from heat.

**Step 5: Second Fry**
Increase the oil temperature to 375°F (190°C). Return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil in batches. Fry for another 3-5 minutes, or until the chicken is deeply golden brown and incredibly crispy. This second fry is key to achieving that signature Korean Fried Chicken crunch. Remove and drain well on the wire rack.

**Step 6: Toss and Serve**
Immediately transfer the hot, crispy chicken to a large bowl. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss quickly until every piece is evenly coated. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions or crushed peanuts if desired. Serve immediately.

## Tips for Perfectly Crispy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken
Achieving that legendary crunch is easier than you think with these expert tips:

*   **Double Frying is Non-Negotiable:** This is the secret weapon. The first fry cooks the chicken through, and the rest period allows moisture to escape. The second, hotter fry crisps up the exterior to perfection.
*   **Dry Chicken is Happy Chicken:** Ensure your chicken is thoroughly patted dry before seasoning and battering. Excess moisture leads to a soggy crust.
*   **The Power of Starch and Baking Powder:** Cornstarch or potato starch, combined with a little baking powder in the batter, creates a lighter, airier, and crispier coating that holds up well.
*   **Maintain Oil Temperature:** Use a thermometer to keep your oil at the correct temperatures for both fries. Fluctuations can lead to greasy or undercooked chicken. Don't overcrowd the pot, as this drops the oil temperature.
*   **Toss Just Before Serving:** To preserve the maximum crispness, toss the hot, freshly fried chicken in the warm Yangnyeom sauce right before you're ready to eat.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings
Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a feast for the senses on its own, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Korean accompaniments. The quintessential pairing is with pickled daikon radish (mu-saengchae), whose refreshing tang cuts through the richness of the chicken beautifully. A simple side of steamed white rice, a crisp green salad, or a creamy coleslaw can also complement the meal. And, of course, no discussion of Korean Fried Chicken is complete without mentioning "chimaek" (치맥) – the beloved combination of chicken and beer. A cold lager or a shot of soju makes for an authentic and satisfying experience.

## Variations and Substitutions
While the classic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a masterpiece, feel free to experiment:

*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of gochujang to your preference. For extra heat, add a pinch of gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) to the sauce.
*   **Sweetness:** Vary the sugar content. Honey or corn syrup can be substituted for granulated sugar for a different texture and flavor.
*   **Vinegar:** While rice vinegar is traditional, apple cider vinegar can be used for a slightly fruitier tang.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Use gluten-free soy sauce and ensure your starch is certified gluten-free.
*   **Vegetarian:** This sauce and frying method can be adapted for crispy fried tofu, cauliflower florets, or mushrooms for a delicious meat-free alternative.
*   **Other Korean Chicken Recipes:** Explore other popular Korean fried chicken sauces like soy garlic (ganjang), honey butter, or spicy gochujang without the sweetness.

## FAQs about Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

**Q: What makes Korean Fried Chicken so crispy?**
A: The primary secret lies in the double-frying technique and the use of starches (like cornstarch or potato starch) in the batter, often combined with baking powder, which creates a lighter, airier, and more resilient crust.

**Q: Is Yangnyeom chicken very spicy?**
A: Yangnyeom sauce has a distinct kick from gochujang, but it's beautifully balanced by sweetness and savory notes. The spice level is usually moderate, but you can easily adjust it by increasing or decreasing the amount of gochujang.

**Q: Can I make the Yangnyeom sauce ahead of time?**
A: Yes, absolutely! You can prepare the sauce days in advance and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to a week. Gently reheat it on the stovetop before tossing with the freshly fried chicken.

**Q: What kind of chicken is best for Yangnyeom?**
A: Bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces like wings and drumsticks are most popular because they retain moisture and flavor well. Boneless, skinless thighs can also be used for a quicker cooking time, but the skin adds significant texture and flavor.

Mastering Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken at home is a rewarding culinary adventure. With its irresistible crunch and perfectly balanced sweet, spicy, and savory glaze, this iconic Korean Chicken Recipe is sure to become a favorite. So, roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and prepare to delight your taste buds with a truly exceptional homemade feast!
